when_to_use.description: Technique pages: when this method earns its place over alternatives.

failure_modes.description: Technique pages: what goes wrong, why, how to recover.

practice_notes.description: Technique pages: extra reps for building the muscle.

density_g_per_ml.description: Ingredient bulk density in grams per ml. Used for mass↔volume display conversions and nutrition. Defaults to 1.0 (water-equivalent) when absent. Examples: AP flour 0.53, granulated sugar 0.85, butter 0.911.

imperial_pref.description: Preferred imperial unit for this ingredient when displayed under imperial mode. Defaults to a size-driven choice. Set 'cup' for flour/sugar where volume is the cook's idiom even when mass-measured.

grams_per_unit.description: Grams in one piece/whole/clove/pod/stick. Lets nutrition compute when a recipe uses unit: 'each' / 'clove' / 'pod' / 'stick'. Examples: large egg 50, garlic clove 4, star-anise pod 0.5, cinnamon stick 3.

pack.description: Default purchase-unit for this ingredient (canned/bottled/boxed). When a recipe references this ingredient, the shopping-list view rolls quantity up into whole packs.

size_ml.description: Volume of one pack in ml. Used with the ingredient's density to compute pack mass.

size_g.description: Mass of one pack in grams (use when packs are sold by mass, not volume).

size_label.description: How a pack reads on the shelf, e.g. '13.5 oz / 400 ml can', '500 g brick', '1 L carton'.

id.description: Internal id within this recipe so derived rows can reference this row via derive_from. Lowercase slug-style.

qty.description: Numeric for scaling; string allowed for 'to taste'

unit.description: g, kg, ml, l, tsp, tbsp, cup, oz, lb, each, pinch

density_g_per_ml.description: Per-line density override. Falls back to the linked ingredient's density, then to water (1.0).

imperial_pref.description: Per-line imperial unit preference. Falls back to the linked ingredient's preference, then to size-driven default.

grams_per_unit.description: Per-line override for grams in one piece/clove/pod/stick. Falls back to the linked ingredient's grams_per_unit.

pack.description: Marks this row as a buying-unit (can/bottle/brick). Inherits size_ml/size_label from the linked ingredient page when set there. Pack rows render as 'N × <label>' in the shopping list and never appear in the per-phase mise breakdown.

derive_from.description: Marks this row as a derived split of another ingredient row (referenced by its id). Derived rows show only in the per-phase mise breakdown — their grams come out of the parent pack and are not double-counted in shopping list or nutrition.

before_you_start.description: Prep coordination and scheduling notes the cook needs BEFORE starting mise-en-place. Use for long-lead steps (steaming sticky rice, soaking beans 8 hours, chilling oil for spherification, proofing dough overnight) and parallel-track instructions (start the rice now so it's done when the meatballs are seared). Renders as a callout above mise. Accepts a single paragraph string OR an array of bullet strings.

substitutions.description: 1:1 ingredient swaps. Use only when the dish stays fundamentally the same dish. For variations that change the dish's character (protein swap with technique adjustment, regional pivot, dietary recasting), use 'modifications' instead.

modifications.description: Genuine pivots: regional variants, dietary recastings, texture or protein swaps that require technique or timing changes. Distinct from 'substitutions' which are direct ingredient-for-ingredient swaps.

to.description: What it becomes. e.g. 'bone-in chicken thighs', 'vegan'.

how.description: What the cook actually has to do differently — quantities, technique, timing.

kind.description: Optional pivot kind for visual grouping.

homemade_alternatives.description: Store-bought ingredients in this recipe that have a reasonable homemade version. Each entry links to a recipe page (or a stub for one) that teaches the homemade form. Different from substitutions — substitutions are 'use this instead'; homemade_alternatives are 'or make it yourself'.

for.description: The store-bought ingredient as it reads in this recipe (e.g. 'chicken stock', 'ricotta').

recipe_slug.description: Recipe path like 'recipes/chicken-stock'. Stub the page if not yet drafted.

why.description: One sentence on why making it yourself is worth the effort (depth, cost, control).

pairings.description: Explicit, author-curated pairings shown ahead of the auto-generated 'Pairs with' candidates. Use for dishes whose canonical companions cannot be inferred from cuisine + course alone (larb + sticky rice, pho + herb plate, curry + jasmine rice). Each entry points at another recipe with a one-sentence reason; auto-generated pairings fill the remaining slots.

reason.description: One sentence on why this pairing earns its place.

homemade_exempt.description: Store-bought ingredients for which there is no reasonable homemade version (e.g. canned coconut milk, kosher salt, fish sauce). Strings here suppress the validate-formatting homemade-alternatives warning for the matching ingredient. Use sparingly — most pantry items DO have a worthwhile homemade form.

safety_notes.description: Annotations on a recipe or technique that flag a temperature, time, or handling call that sits at or below USDA published-safe targets (or otherwise carries a non-obvious safety dimension). Each entry points at a section in content/safety/ that explains the reasoning. The build renders these as a Safety callout block; the validate-safety validator surfaces flagged temperatures that are missing a safety_notes entry.

for.description: What the note covers as the recipe or technique states it. e.g. 'pulling chicken at 145°F', 'raw yolk in carbonara', '2-day fridge holding for tea eggs'.

why.description: One sentence on why this call is safe (or what extra care it demands). The line shown to readers.

hero_image.description: Optional override of the hero image filename. Default convention: content/images/recipes/<slug>/hero.jpg auto-detected.

members.description: For hub pages: curated recipe collections
